title: limonium pruinosum subsp. alleizettei
intro: Limonium pruinosum subsp. alleizettei is a type of flowering plant, also known as sea lavender, that belongs to the family Plumbaginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Limonium pruinosum subsp. alleizettei include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be made by drying the flowers and leaves, or by infusing them in oil.
